translation: # Create a CSVFormat object with LF as the record separator
# Assert that the record separator is equal to LF
# Create a CSVFormat object with record separator as CR
# Assert that the record separator is equal to the string representation of CR
assert csvFormatTwo.toString() == "Delimiter=<,> QuoteChar=<\"> CommentStart=<n> RecordSeparator=<\r\n> EmptyLines:ignored SkipHeaderRecord:false"
self.assertNotEqual(csv_format_two, csv_format)  # CSV-244 - should not be equal

body:
public void testEqualsSkipHeaderRecord() {
        final CSVFormat right =
                CSVFormat.newFormat('\'')
                        .builder()
                        .setRecordSeparator0(CR)
                        .setCommentMarker0('#')
                        .setEscape0('+')
                        .setIgnoreEmptyLines(true)
                        .setIgnoreSurroundingSpaces(true)
                        .setQuote0('"')
                        .setQuoteMode(QuoteMode.ALL)
                        .setNullString("null")
                        .setSkipHeaderRecord(true)
                        .build();
        final CSVFormat left = right.builder().setSkipHeaderRecord(false).build();

        assertNotEquals0(right, left);
    }

translation: def __assertNotEquals1(self, name: str, type: str, left: typing.Any, right: typing.Any) -> None:
if left == right or right == left:
self.fail("Objects must not compare equal for " + name + "(" + type + ")")
self.fail("Hash code should not be equal for " + name + "(" + type + ")")
assert right != left, f"{right} should not equal {left}"
assert left != right, f"{left} should not equal {right}"
